speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Help me, my women! O, he’s more mad

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Than Telamon for his shield; the boar of Thessaly

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: There lock yourself and send him word you are dead.

speaker: CHARMIAN
text: The soul and body rive not more in parting

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Mardian, go tell him I have slain myself.

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: Say that the last I spoke was “Antony,”

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And word it, prithee, piteously. Hence, Mardian,

speaker: CLEOPATRA
text: And bring me how he takes my death.—To th’ monument!
